summary: Keep the HTTP MCP transport loopback-only by default and require explicit confirmation before the generic executor invokes a mutating endpoint.
reason: The MCP server can use the operator's Discogs token, so a public default listener or an unconfirmed generic POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE call could expose authenticated remote mutations beyond the CLI's safety boundary.
validated_outcome: HTTP defaults to 127.0.0.1:7777; discogs_execute advertises destructiveHint=true and blocks non-read endpoints unless confirm=true.
